title: Anthropic Files Confidential IPO Draft as AI Valuation Nears $1 Trillion
detail:
Anthropic Files Confidential IPO Draft as AI Valuation Nears $1 Trillion
CryptoProwl
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 12:53 PM MST 2 min read
ANTH.PVT
GOOG
AVGO
AMZN
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Anthropic has taken its first formal step toward a public listing, confidentially submitting a draft S-1 registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission as the Claude maker moves closer to the public-market stage.

The filing does not guarantee an IPO. Anthropic said the proposed offering would depend on SEC review, market conditions and other factors, and that the number of shares and price range have not been set. The confidential process lets the company begin the regulatory review before deciding whether to launch a roadshow.

The timing keeps Anthropic at the center of a widening AI capital cycle. Just days earlier, the company said it raised $65 billion in Series H funding at a $965 billion post-money valuation, placing it near the trillion-dollar threshold while private-market investors continue to chase exposure to frontier AI.

More From Cryptoprowl:

Ripple, The Company Behind XRP, Is Valued At $50 Billion

Eightco Secures $125 Million Investment From Bitmine And ARK Invest, Shares Surge

Blockchain Projects Decline 75% As Developers Shift To A.I.

Stanley Druckenmiller Says Stablecoins Could Reshape Global Finance

New York Stock Exchange Invests $600 Million In Polymarket

The round was led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks and Sequoia Capital, with additional backing from large institutional and strategic investors. Anthropic said run-rate revenue crossed $47 billion earlier in May, helped by enterprise adoption of Claude and broader use of its tools in everyday work.

CFO Krishna Rao said the funding would help Anthropic “serve the historic demand” for Claude while expanding research, compute and product partnerships. That language points to the same pressure shaping the next wave of AI listings: investors are looking for revenue growth, but the infrastructure bill behind that growth is also expanding quickly.

Anthropic has recently signed major compute agreements with Amazon (NASDAQ: $AMZN), Google (NASDAQ: $GOOGL) and Broadcom (NASDAQ: $AVGO), and SpaceX-linked capacity for Colossus 1 and Colossus 2. The company says Claude is available across AWS, Google Cloud and Microsoft Azure, giving it distribution through the largest cloud platforms.

A public listing would give investors a clearer look at Anthropic’s revenue quality, spending profile and compute commitments. It would also test how much appetite remains for frontier AI exposure after one of the largest private funding rounds in technology.

title: Anthropic Files Confidential S-1 as $1 Trillion IPO Window Opens
detail:
Anthropic Files Confidential S-1 as $1 Trillion IPO Window Opens
Renato Neves, CFA
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 11:12 AM MST 1 min read
ANTH.PVT
SPCX
GOOG
AMZN
SPAX.PVT
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Anthropic, PBC submitted a confidential draft Form S-1 to the SEC on June 1, giving the company the option to go public once regulators complete their review. The filing does not set a share count or price, and the company noted the offering remains subject to market conditions. No timeline has been disclosed.

The filing comes four days after the company closed a $65 billion Series H at a $965 billion post-money valuation, led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital. Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) and Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) are among Anthropic's existing strategic investors. That puts Anthropic just short of a $1 trillion valuation before it has sold a single public share.

Anthropic's S-1 follows SpaceX, which filed publicly with the SEC in late May. OpenAI is also preparing to file confidentially for a U.S. initial public offering in the coming weeks. All three companies could be valued at more than $1 trillion at listing, making this one of the most concentrated windows of landmark IPO activity in years.

title: Amazon’s satellite internet project seen as significant revenue opportunity by Bank of America
detail:
Amazon’s satellite internet project seen as significant revenue opportunity by Bank of America
Amazon’s satellite internet project seen as significant revenue opportunity by Bank of America Proactive uses images sourced from Shutterstock
Proactive
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 10:43 AM MST 2 min read
AMZN
BAC
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Amazon.com Inc (NASDAQ:AMZN)’s low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite initiative is approaching a key operational milestone as deployment activity accelerates and commercial service moves closer to launch, according to research from Bank of America.

The firm wrote that Amazon’s satellite project is "on the cusp of generating revenues," with satellite launches accelerating during the second quarter and commercial service expected to begin in the third quarter.

Bank of America expects investor interest in the initiative to increase as the network build-out progresses.

Amazon has completed three satellite launches during the second quarter and had more than 300 satellites in orbit as of mid-May. Bank of America expects the launch pace to continue accelerating, projecting four launches in the second quarter and 17 launches in 2026, with additional launches planned for 2027.

The bank estimated Amazon could invest approximately $25 billion through 2028 to build its initial satellite constellation, excluding consumer equipment costs.

It also forecast direct income statement expenses related to the project of about $1.3 billion in the second quarter and $1.6 billion in the third quarter. Those costs are expected to decline sequentially in the fourth quarter as Amazon begins capitalizing production and launch expenses.

Bank of America wrote that while consumer broadband subscriptions remain a core component of the business case, enterprise, government and direct-to-device (D2D) mobile services could ultimately generate revenue comparable to or greater than consumer offerings.

The analysts highlighted customer agreements involving Delta Air Lines, the US Department of Defense, Airbus and Apple. Bank of America also pointed to Amazon’s recent acquisition of Globalstar and the company’s plans to begin mobile satellite deployments in 2028.

Looking ahead, the firm estimated Amazon’s consumer broadband business could generate approximately $14 billion in annual revenue by 2032, assuming the company captures a 25% share of the consumer satellite internet market. Enterprise and government services could contribute an additional $6 billion to $11 billion in annual revenue, according to the projections.

Bank of America also identified potential synergies between the satellite network and Amazon’s existing businesses, including Amazon Web Services and the company’s logistics operations.

The firm reiterated its ‘Buy’ rating on Amazon, citing what it views as a compelling long-term opportunity with the potential for positive returns on investment and strong margins supported by high barriers to entry in the satellite communications market.

Shares of Amazon traded down 3% at about $262 on Monday afternoon.

title: Jim Cramer Notes “Snowflake Understands the Transition to AI”
detail:
Jim Cramer Notes “Snowflake Understands the Transition to AI”
Syeda Seirut Javed
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 10:33 AM MST 2 min read
SNOW
AMZN
ANTH.PVT
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Snowflake Inc. (NYSE:SNOW) was one of the stocks on Jim Cramer’s radar on Mad Money as he explained that many investors might be missing out on the market’s biggest winners. Cramer noted the company’s AI journey, as he remarked:

Let’s dive into how Snowflake dazzled so brightly just all session. How did it do it? Okay, as CEO Sridhar Ramaswamy explained on our show last night, the company figured out how to pivot from plain old software to a terrific amalgam of software and artificial intelligence supported by partnerships with Anthropic, which, by the way, just announced today that it raised $65 billion at a post-money valuation of $965 billion.

How incredible is that? It’s also partnered with OpenAI and then the web services of Amazon. With its quarter last night, Snowflake gave the latter $6 billion to have Amazon’s homemade chips. That’s right, Amazon isn’t giving Snowflake $6 billion; Snowflake will be giving $6 billion to Amazon. And that was a huge part of the positive story. It showed you that Snowflake understands the transition to AI. They get it. I like that.

Stock market data. Photo by Photo by Alesia Kozik

Snowflake Inc. (NYSE:SNOW) provides a cloud platform that helps organizations pull their data into one place so they can analyze it, build data apps, share information, and use AI to solve business challenges.

While we acknowledge the potential of SNOW as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL): One of the Premier Stock Picks of Jeff Bezos
detail:
Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL): One of the Premier Stock Picks of Jeff Bezos
Usman Kabir
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 10:13 AM MST 2 min read
GOOG
AMZN
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

We just covered the Jeff Bezos Stock Portfolio: Top 10 Stock Picks and Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) ranks 9th on this list.

Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) has had a historic and highly celebrated relationship with Jeff Bezos, originating well before the former transitioned into its modern holding company structure. In 1998, just four years after launching Amazon, Bezos aggressively negotiated with Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin to let him become one of the search engine’s earliest angel investors. Facilitated by board member Kavitark Ram Shriram, Bezos personally injected $250,000 into the fledgling startup while it was still operating out of a rented garage. That early check secured millions of shares at a split-adjusted cost of roughly four cents apiece. While it remains unknown exactly how much of his stake Bezos held following Google’s blockbuster 2004 initial public offering, the bet is widely regarded as one of the most successful venture capital placements in technology history.

READ ALSO: Mark Cuban Stock Portfolio: 8 Best Stocks to Buy.

Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) had a stellar Q1 2026 earnings performance, which highlighted an accelerating dual-engine growth narrative driven by advertising resilience and cloud scaling. Total revenue for the quarter jumped 22% year-over-year to $109.9 billion, while net income surged to $62.8 billion, delivering an adjusted EPS that comfortably beat Wall Street consensus. Furthermore, Alphabet’s core search and YouTube advertising segments exhibited pricing power, capturing a dominant share of global digital ad spend. Operating margins expanded by more than 36%, fueled by hardware efficiencies.

While we acknowledge the potential of GOOGL as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. We just covered the Jeff Bezos Stock Portfolio: Top 10 Stock Picks and Remitly Global, Inc. (NASDAQ:RELY) ranks 7th on this list.

Jeff Bezos’s relationship with Remitly Global, Inc. (NASDAQ:RELY) began during the digital remittance platform’s formative startup phase over a decade ago. In 2012, Bezos’s personal investment arm, Bezos Expeditions, stepped in as a premier backer for Remitly’s $2.6 million Series A funding round, discovering the company through the Techstars network and co-founder Josh Hug. Remitly was built to disrupt traditional legacy money transfer giants by leveraging a mobile-first, cloud-native architecture that slashes cross-border payment friction for immigrant communities. Co-founder Matt Oppenheimer frequently cited Amazon’s customer-obsessed operational framework as the definitive blueprint for managing Remitly’s regulatory and logistical complexities. Bezos’s early capital injection provided the critical infrastructure funding required to map thousands of secure, compliant financial pathways globally.

Jeff Bezos Investments in 2024: Companies Bezos Is Investing In

Remitly Global, Inc. (NASDAQ:RELY) had an impressive Q1 2026 financial performance. The firm recorded total quarterly revenue of $452.8 million, up from $361.6 million in Q1 2025, driven by an expanding active customer base that reached 9.4 million users. The company achieved profitability milestones, generating a net income of $49.1 million and expanding its adjusted EBITDA by 74% year-over-year to $101.6 million. Remitly’s premium mobile platform continues to outpace brick-and-mortar competitors, maintaining a highly sticky user retention rate and excellent customer lifetime value.

In the summer of 2011, Bezos Expeditions participated heavily in the transformative $112 million Series B funding round for Airbnb, Inc. (NASDAQ:ABNB), which valued the nascent home-sharing platform at just over $1 billion. Led by venture firm Andreessen Horowitz, this capital injection occurred at a time when Airbnb was shifting from a quirky marketplace for couch-surfers into a legitimate global travel alternative. Bezos recognized that Airbnb’s model, which aggregates global lodging inventory without owning a single piece of physical real estate, closely mirrored Amazon’s digital marketplace dynamics. His early funding provided the financial cushion required for Airbnb to expand its international operational teams, navigate complex local regulatory hurdles, and scale its global trust and safety frameworks.

Here's Why Jim Cramer Is Happy With AirBnb (ABNB)

Total nights and experiences booked expanded by 11% year-over-year, driven by an accelerating recovery across the Asia-Pacific region and cross-border travel trends. Airbnb’s unique business model allowed it to convert a major chunk of its revenue directly into free cash flow, generating an impressive $1.7 billion during the quarter.

title: Blue Origin Setback Tests Amazon Kuiper Ambitions And Airline Connectivity Plans
detail:
Blue Origin Setback Tests Amazon Kuiper Ambitions And Airline Connectivity Plans
Simply Wall St
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 9:07 AM MST 4 min read
AMZN
DAL
SPAX.PVT
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket recently exploded during testing, creating new delays for Amazon.com's Project Kuiper satellite rollout.

The setback raises questions about the pace and reliability of Amazon's satellite deployments compared with competitors in global broadband.

At the same time, Amazon's satellite broadband unit, Amazon Leo, has secured key airline deals, including Delta Air Lines choosing it for in flight Wi Fi over SpaceX's Starlink.

For investors watching NasdaqGS:AMZN at a share price of $270.64, these space related developments add another layer to an already complex business. The stock is up 19.5% year to date and 31.0% over the past year, while the 3 year return stands at 116.0%. This provides context for how the market has treated Amazon over multiple time frames.

The Blue Origin setback and Amazon Leo airline wins highlight both execution risk and opportunity around Amazon's satellite connectivity plans. Readers may want to track how Amazon reallocates launches, communicates revised Kuiper timelines, and integrates airline partnerships into its broader connectivity and cloud ambitions.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Amazon.com by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Amazon.com.

NasdaqGS:AMZN Earnings & Revenue Growth as at Jun 2026

We've flagged 1 risk for Amazon.com. See which could impact your investment.

The New Glenn explosion puts a spotlight on execution risk for Project Kuiper, because Amazon depends on launch partners to get its low Earth orbit satellites into space on schedule. Any delay to Kuiper can slow Amazon Leo’s ability to serve airlines and enterprise customers at scale, which matters when competing with SpaceX’s Starlink and OneWeb in global broadband. At the same time, securing long term in flight Wi Fi deals with Delta and Apple backed hardware integration suggests airlines see value in Amazon’s broader tech stack, from connectivity to cloud and data services. For you, the tension is clear: physical launch setbacks can hold back capacity growth, while commercial wins indicate customer demand for Amazon powered connectivity that plugs into AWS and its AI tools.

How This Fits Into The Amazon.com Narrative

The airline contracts support the existing narrative that Amazon is using infrastructure businesses such as Project Kuiper to widen AWS and Prime ecosystems, by turning connectivity into another on ramp for cloud and data services.

The Blue Origin delay pushes against the narrative’s assumption that large scale infrastructure projects, including satellites, translate smoothly into higher margin growth, because launch failures can stretch timelines and capital needs.

The narrative highlights AI infrastructure and logistics but does not directly factor in launch provider dependence, which means reliability and pricing of partners like Blue Origin, SpaceX or others may not be fully reflected in the story.

title: Prediction: Broadcom's June 3 Earnings Report Will Matter More Than Any "Magnificent Seven" Stock This Quarter
detail:
Prediction: Broadcom's June 3 Earnings Report Will Matter More Than Any "Magnificent Seven" Stock This Quarter
Daniel Foelber, The Motley Fool
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 7:55 AM MST 4 min read
NVDA
AVGO
GOOG
AMZN
AAPL
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

The "Magnificent Seven" -- Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA), Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG), Apple, Microsoft, Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN), Tesla, and Meta Platforms -- collectively make up roughly 35% of the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC). These companies can certainly move markets with their quarterly earnings reports. But as investors saw from the most valuable member of the group, Nvidia, Wall Street's response to earnings can be relatively calm.

Nvidia delivered better-than-expected top- and bottom-line growth, announced a new $80 billion stock repurchase program, and a 2,400% increase in the quarterly dividend, and yet the stock barely budged -- down less than 2% the day after the report.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO), which isn't technically in the Magnificent Seven despite being worth more than Tesla and Meta, will report its second-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings on June 3. Here's why its report matters more than any of the other Magnificent Seven companies.

Image source: Getty Images.
Broadcom is on the cutting edge of AI investment

A staggering 92% of Nvidia's revenue from its most recent quarter came from data centers -- driven by artificial intelligence (AI) demand for compute. Similarly, Broadcom is rapidly growing its AI semiconductor business, but it is also an AI networking powerhouse -- guiding for 40% of total AI revenue in its upcoming quarter to come from networking -- not chips.

While Nvidia is the leader in graphics processing units (GPUs), Broadcom specializes in designing AI accelerators, called XPUs. These chips are built for specific use cases, enabling cost savings and efficiency improvements at scale.

Broadcom partners with Alphabet to design Google's Tensor Processing Units (TPUs). Google recently announced two new TPUs, the 8t for training and the 8i for inference. And on Broadcom's March earnings call, CEO Hock Tan discussed in detail the need for separate custom chips for AI inference and training, and forecasted that XPUs will ultimately overtake the GPU design in data centers.

On Amazon and Alphabet's latest earnings calls, both companies discussed their booming chip businesses, with Alphabet saying it may begin selling TPUs to select third parties and Amazon stating that if its chip business were a standalone entity, and it sold chips to third parties, it would have $50 billion in annual run rate revenue.
